sections.biography-source-undefined: Cao Xi was a Cao Wei noble who was the son of Cao Zhen and the younger brother of Cao Shuang. He held the title of Marquis of Anxiang 安鄉侯. He tried to moderate the actions of Cao Shuang, recognizing that he was attracting many enemies, but he failed. Cao Xi would be right when Sima Yi launched an effective coup against Cao Shuang with the support of the disgruntled court officials. Cao Xi lost his power and was soon executed on charges of treason.

full_text:
Cao Xi (Zhaoshu) 曹羲 (昭叔)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Xi (曹羲), styledZhaoshu (昭叔). Warned Cao Shuang about his excesses but was ignored. Recommended surrender to Sima Yi. Cao Xi was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Cao Xi lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

Cao Xiu’s Biography

BiographySource Undefined
Cao Xi was a Cao Wei noble who was the son of Cao Zhen and the younger brother of Cao Shuang. He held the title of Marquis of Anxiang 安鄉侯. He tried to moderate the actions of Cao Shuang, recognizing that he was attracting many enemies, but he failed. Cao Xi would be right when Sima Yi launched an effective coup against Cao Shuang with the support of the disgruntled court officials. Cao Xi lost his power and was soon executed on charges of treason.
